description_long: This object is a large, ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels arranged in a staggered, angular configuration. The panels are set on a broad, flat base structure, likely made of metal, with visible support struts and raised sections to optimize sunlight capture. The surface of the panels is dark and reflective, typical of photovoltaic cells, while the base is a lighter metallic color with grid-like texturing. The installation includes several elevated and sloped panel arrays, suggesting a design for maximum solar exposure. The structure appears robust and industrial, suitable for placement in urban environments such as parking lots, rooftops, or open public spaces. No visible cabling or inverter housings are apparent, and the mounting system is integrated into the base platform.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar panel array with multiple angled panels on a sturdy metallic base, designed for urban solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Front view showing the staggered heights and angular arrangement of the solar panels on a wide, textured base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped orientation of the panels and the elongated, low-profile base structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back edges of the panels and the supporting base, with less detail on the panel surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the angular placement of the panels and the overall length of the installation.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 25-35 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly specified.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with integrated metallic base, likely aluminum or steel frame.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows of panels in a staggered, angular configuration, approximately 6-8 panels total.
